disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe lumbar/pelvic muscle spasm triggered by standing after prolonged sitting, in a patient with a known long-standing inflammatory back condition.
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when the pain started?
Tell me how this came on.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'd been sitting on the sofa for ages, went to stand up, and my whole lower back just seized into a spasm and locked me up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain is a deep, bilateral, locked ache across the low back and sacroiliac region; there is NO sharp electric pain shooting down the leg past the knee.
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain shoot down into your leg or foot?
Is it a deep ache in the back, or a sharp electric pain travelling down the leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't shoot down my leg — it's a deep, locked ache right across the base of my back and both sides, it stays there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No loss of bowel or bladder control and passing urine normally (cauda equina screen — negative on history).
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any accidents with your bladder or bowels, or trouble passing urine?
Any loss of control of your bladder or bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — my bladder and bowels are working completely normally.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No numbness in the saddle area — around the groin, genitals or buttocks (cauda equina screen — negative on history).
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness or altered feeling around your groin, genitals or the area you'd sit on?
Is there any numbness between your legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no numbness down there at all — that all feels normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new weakness in the legs and no sense of the legs giving way — the difficulty moving is because of pain and spasm, not loss of power (cauda equina / cord screen — negative on history).
trigger_question_examples: Are your legs weak or giving way, or is it the pain stopping you moving?
Can you feel and move both legs normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My legs feel fine and strong — I can feel and move them okay, it's the back pain that stops me. They're not giving way.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background pattern: the back is classically stiff in the mornings and loosens with movement; the condition is HLA-B27 positive with normal plain radiographs historically.
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about your usual back problem — what's it normally like?
Is your back typically worse in the mornings or after rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Normally it's stiff and sore first thing in the morning and eases off once I get moving. They've told me it's an inflammatory type, HLA-B27 positive, and my X-rays have always been normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent significant trauma, fall or injury, and no known osteoporosis or fragility-fracture history (vertebral fracture screen — negative on history).
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any fall, injury or heavy lift before this?
Any history of thin bones or previous fractures?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No injury or fall — I just stood up. And no, I've never been told I have thin bones or had a fracture.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient lives alone and is currently unable to mobilise safely, including reaching the bathroom.
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone with you at home?
Are you able to move around safely right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and right now I can't get out of the chair, so I'm worried about even getting to the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is frightened the pain means a slipped/ruptured disc or spinal injury and is seeking urgent reassurance.
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
Is there something in particular you're afraid is happening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm scared I've slipped a disc or damaged my spine and that I might do permanent damage if I move.
